heading: Vessel escort operations and towing assistance
label: 55112 Vessel escort operations and towing assistance
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)5511246 App.:316a., , .Pub. L. 107–295, title IV, § 404116 Stat. 2114Nov. 25, 2002In subsection (a), the words “(as that term is defined in , United States Code)” are omitted because the definition of “vessel of the United States” is being moved from section 2101 to chapter 1 of the revised title and will apply title-wide.section 2101 of title 46
Amendments2020—Subsec. (a)(3). substituted “Great Lakes St. Lawrence Seaway Development Corporation” for “St. Lawrence Seaway Development Corporation”.Pub. L. 116–260

L. 107–295, title IV, § 404116 Stat. 2114Nov. 25, 2002In subsection (a), the words “(as that term is defined in , United States Code)” are omitted because the definition of “vessel of the United States” is being moved from section 2101 to chapter 1 of the revised title and will apply title-wide.section 2101 of title 46
Amendments2020—Subsec. (a)(3). substituted “Great Lakes St. Lawrence Seaway Development Corporation” for “St. Lawrence Seaway Development Corporation”.Pub. L. 116–260

children.text: Operations that commence or terminate at a port or place in the United States.
Operations required by United States law or regulation.

Operations provided in whole or in part within or through navigation facilities owned, maintained, or operated by the United States Government or the approaches to those facilities, other than facilities operated by the Great Lakes St. Lawrence Seaway Development Corporation on the St. Lawrence River portion of the Seaway.

any vessel that is assigned and dedicated to assist another vessel, whether or not tethered to that vessel, solely as a safety precaution to assist in controlling the speed or course of the assisted vessel in the event of a steering or propulsion equipment failure, or any other similar emergency circumstance, or in restricted waters where additional assistance in maneuvering the vessel is required to ensure its safe operation; and

in the case of a vessel being towed under , any vessel that is assigned and dedicated to the vessel being towed in addition to any towing vessel required under that section.section 55111 of this title

children.heading: .—Relationship to Other Law
children.label: c .—Relationship to Other Law
children.text: This section does not affect .section 55111 of this title

children.heading: .—Penalty
children.label: d .—Penalty
children.text: A person violating this section is liable to the Government for a civil penalty of not more than $10,000 for each day during which the violation occurs.
